content.text: This is a Gleam compiler written in Rust. The bug is about a "Add missing patterns" code action that removes comments in `case` statements. Let me search for relevant code related to this

content.content:
Found 5 files
changelog/v1.11.md
changelog/v1.14.md
changelog/v1.15.md
language-server/src/code_action.rs
language-server/src/tests/action.rs
File content (375.8KB) exceeds maximum allowed size (256KB). Use offset and limit parameters to read specific portions of the file, or search for specific content instead of reading the whole file.

tool_use_result: Error: File content (375.8KB) exceeds maximum allowed size (256KB). Use offset and limit parameters to read specific portions of the file, or search for specific content instead of reading the whole file.

content.text:
Now I can see the key issue. Look at lines 1284-1303. When there are no clauses (`clauses.is_empty()`), the code:
1. Deletes everything between `{` and the end location (which removes comments!)
2. Inserts a newline with indentation

The fix should instead only remove blank spaces/lines but preserve comments. Let me look at the `TextEdits` struct to understand how `delete` works:
